stemHtml: Which of the following options correctly describes the difference between radiation fog and advection fog?

options.a: Radiation fog forms due to surface cooling at night in a light wind. Advection fog forms when warm humid air flows over a cold surface.
options.b: Radiation fog is formed by surface cooling in a calm wind. Advection fog is formed by evaporation over the sea.
options.c: Radiation fog forms due to night cooling and advection fog due to daytime cooling.
options.d: Radiation fog forms only on the ground, advection fog only on the sea.
